project_type: air-gapped legal document lifecycle management system
prompt:
A legal document lifecycle management system that includes secure identity authentication, role-based document manipulation, forensic redaction workflows, version-controlled PDF processing, structured audit trails, compliance-grade notification delivery, and parameterized system configuration.

IronPage PDF Studio serves as the backbone API for legal professionals, paralegals, and compliance teams who handle sensitive contracts, regulatory filings, and confidential documents within entirely air-gapped environments. The backend is built in Go using the Echo framework for RESTful API routing, sqlx for type-safe database interaction, and PostgreSQL as the sole persistence layer for metadata, audit records, configuration dictionaries, and notification queues, while PDF binary assets reside on the local filesystem referenced by database pointers. The system governs three discrete roles — Admin, Editor, and Reviewer — each isolated through strict RBAC enforcement at the API capability boundary. Admins manage user accounts, system configuration dictionaries, workflow status definitions, and notification templates. Editors hold full document manipulation authority including upload, batch import of up to 250 files per operation, version rollback across a 50-revision history ceiling, redaction confirmation, Bates numbering application, and document finalization. Reviewers may only retrieve documents, attach annotations (sticky notes, highlights, strikethroughs, and freeform text stamps, each carrying author attribution, timestamp, and an optional comment capped at 2,000 characters), flag annotation dispositions as Approved, Rejected, or Needs Discussion, and advance documents through the review workflow. Documents progress through a mandatory status chain — Draft, Under Review, Redaction Pending, Approved, Finalized — where Finalized status renders the record permanently immutable. The redaction subsystem enforces a two-phase commit: proposed redaction regions are staged as coordinate-bound metadata, and only upon an authorized Editor's burn-in confirmation are the underlying content regions permanently stripped from the binary. Bates numbering supports custom prefixes, suffixes, configurable zero-padding up to ten digits, and sequential application across batch document sets. A document comparison capability accepts two version identifiers and returns a structured diff report enumerating added, removed, and modified text segments with page numbers and bounding coordinates.

Authentication relies exclusively on local username-and-password verification with bcrypt-hashed credentials stored in PostgreSQL, and accounts lock automatically for fifteen minutes after five consecutive failed attempts within a rolling window. Sessions are managed through locally signed JWT tokens that expire after eight hours of inactivity, with anti-replay protection enforced via a server-side token jti blacklist and request timestamp validation rejecting payloads older than sixty seconds. All sensitive fields — including password hashes, personally identifiable metadata, and redaction coordinates — are encrypted at rest using AES-256 column-level encryption, while API responses apply contextual data masking based on the caller's role. The audit log subsystem records every mutating action — document uploads, version changes, redaction proposals and confirmations, workflow transitions, annotation creation, user management operations — capturing the acting user, target document identifier, action type, and timestamp, with indefinite retention and support for filtered queries across user, document, action type, and date range dimensions. An internal notification queue generates in-app message records upon workflow status transitions and annotation mentions, queryable per user with a 500-record unread ceiling and explicit read-acknowledgment capability. Notification templates and workflow status definitions are stored as parameterized configuration entries editable by Admins, enabling teams to customize naming conventions without code changes. All paginated API responses default to twenty-five items with a hard maximum of one hundred per page, and every error response follows a uniform envelope structure. PDF files are constrained to a maximum of 500 pages and 200 MB per document, validated through local format inspection and file-size checks at ingestion time. A configurable backup strategy supports scheduled PostgreSQL logical dumps and filesystem snapshots to a designated local volume, with point-in-time recovery documentation maintained as part of the system configuration center. The entire service is packaged as a single Docker container deployable on a standalone machine with no external network dependencies whatsoever.
